req.title: Export employee directory — variant 3
req.statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three concerns: (1) correct CSV export with PII redaction for opted-out employees, (2) webhook emission on export, and (3) access-control and rate-limit error paths. I selected EP for the happy-path (valid hr-admin gets correct CSV + webhook), NEG for the 403 unauthorised path, NEG for the 429 rate-limit path, BVA for the edge of exactly one opted-out vs zero opted-out employees, and DT for the decision matrix combining opt-out flag with expected field redaction. This gives five tests (well above the minimum three) covering all stated error pathways and the variant-3 semantics.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_and_webhook_emitted
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role hr-admin
At least one employee has opted out of public directory
At least one employee has not opted out
Webhook receiver is reachable and recorded
actions: Capture response body as CSV text
Poll webhook receiver for emitted event
expected: HTTP 200 with Content-Type text/csv or application/octet-stream
CSV contains a row for every employee
Opted-out employees have PII fields (email, phone, address) replaced with REDACTED
Non-opted-out employees have full PII present
Webhook receiver recorded exactly one event with type employee_directory_exported
trace: R-WEB-084: hr-admin can export; PII redacted per opt-out; webhook emitted
tests.executable:
import pytest
import csv, io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_client, webhook_receiver, seed_employees

def test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_and_webhook_emitted(
    hr_admin_client, webhook_receiver, seed_employees
):
    opted_out_id, active_id = seed_employees(opted_out=1, active=1)
    resp = hr_admin_client.get("/api/employees/export.csv")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert "csv" in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "").lower() or \
           "octet" in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "").lower()

    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    rows = {r["id"]: r for r in reader}

    assert opted_out_id in rows
    for pii_field in ("email", "phone", "address"):
        assert rows[opted_out_id][pii_field] == "REDACTED", \
            f"{pii_field} must be REDACTED for opted-out employee"

    assert active_id in rows
    for pii_field in ("email", "phone", "address"):
        assert rows[active_id][pii_field] != "REDACTED", \
            f"{pii_field} must not be REDACTED for non-opted-out employee"

    events = webhook_receiver.get_events(event_type="employee_directory_exported")
    assert len(events) == 1, "Exactly one webhook event must be emitted"

tests.name: test_non_admin_export_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role employee (not hr-admin)
Employee directory has at least one record
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden
Response body contains an error indicating insufficient permissions
No CSV data is returned
No webhook event is emitted
trace: R-WEB-084 error pathway: 403 if not hr-admin
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import employee_client, webhook_receiver

def test_non_admin_export_returns_403(employee_client, webhook_receiver):
    resp = employee_client.get("/api/employees/export.csv")
    assert resp.status_code == 403

    body = resp.json() if resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "").startswith("application/json") \
           else {}
    assert body.get("error") or body.get("message"), \
        "Error body must describe the permission failure"

    assert "id," not in resp.text, "CSV data must not be present in 403 response"

    events = webhook_receiver.get_events(event_type="employee_directory_exported")
    assert len(events) == 0, "No webhook must fire on a rejected request"

tests.name: test_rate_limited_export_returns_429
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role hr-admin
Rate-limit threshold for /api/employees/export.csv is known (N requests per window)
Request counter has been exhausted via <helper_fn> exhaust_rate_limit
actions: Exhaust the rate-limit allowance for the hr-admin token
expected: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Response includes Retry-After header
No CSV payload returned
No webhook event emitted for the rejected call
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixture> import hr_admin_client, webhook_receiver
from <helper_fn> import exhaust_rate_limit

RATE_LIMIT_ENDPOINT = "/api/employees/export.csv"

def test_rate_limited_export_returns_429(hr_admin_client, webhook_receiver):
    exhaust_rate_limit(hr_admin_client, RATE_LIMIT_ENDPOINT)

    resp = hr_admin_client.get(RATE_LIMIT_ENDPOINT)
    assert resp.status_code == 429

    assert "Retry-After" in resp.headers, \
        "429 response must include Retry-After header"

    assert "id," not in resp.text, "No CSV data must leak on 429 response"

    events = webhook_receiver.get_events(event_type="employee_directory_exported")
    rate_limited_events = [
        e for e in events if e.get("status") != "rejected"
    ]
    assert len(rate_limited_events) == 0, \
        "Webhook must not fire for a rate-limited request"

tests.name: test_export_with_exactly_one_opted_out_employee
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role hr-admin
Directory seeded with exactly one opted-out employee and zero non-opted-out employees
expected: HTTP 200
CSV contains exactly one data row
All PII fields for that row are REDACTED
Non-PII fields (id, department, job_title) are present and non-empty
Webhook event is emitted
trace: R-WEB-084: BVA on minimum opted-out population (boundary = 1); PII redacted
tests.executable:
import pytest
import csv, io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_client, webhook_receiver, seed_employees

PII_FIELDS = ("email", "phone", "address")
NON_PII_FIELDS = ("id", "department", "job_title")

def test_export_with_exactly_one_opted_out_employee(
    hr_admin_client, webhook_receiver, seed_employees
):
    (opted_out_id,) = seed_employees(opted_out=1, active=0)

    resp = hr_admin_client.get("/api/employees/export.csv")
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    rows = list(csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text)))
    assert len(rows) == 1, "Exactly one data row expected"

    row = rows[0]
    assert row["id"] == opted_out_id

    for field in PII_FIELDS:
        assert row[field] == "REDACTED", f"{field} must be REDACTED"

    for field in NON_PII_FIELDS:
        assert row.get(field), f"{field} must be present and non-empty"

    events = webhook_receiver.get_events(event_type="employee_directory_exported")
    assert len(events) == 1

tests.name: test_opt_out_flag_determines_pii_redaction_decision_table
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role hr-admin
Directory seeded with multiple employees covering all combinations: opted-out=true, opted-out=false
actions: Parse CSV and map each row to its opted-out status
expected: opted_out=true  => all PII fields equal REDACTED
opted_out=false => all PII fields are non-empty real values
Non-PII fields are populated regardless of opt-out status
HTTP 200 returned
trace: R-WEB-084: DT covering opt-out=true/false vs PII present/redacted
